$comment: One-line vocabulary index for the workflow-architect template library. Read by the LLM when picking which idiom to copy. Templates here are not auto-seeded into a draft — they are reference patterns. Each entry lists the file, the intent it matches, and the minimal SpEL/wiring features it demonstrates.

templates.intent: Sequential workflow: fetch -> transform -> produce a result.
Branch on a value: if condition X, go path A; otherwise path B.
demonstrates: route activity with two ON_SUCCESS transitions

templates.intent: Run several independent steps in parallel, then join when all finish.
demonstrates: AND-split with multiple ON_SUCCESS transitions from one route

templates.intent: Aggregate the outputs of several tool calls and summarize via an LLM.
Call an HTTP service with a fallback path when the call fails.
Invoke a tool hosted on an MCP server.
Run a sandboxed Java snippet for data transformation; access result via .output envelope.
demonstrates: code body that returns a Map

templates.intent: Iterate over a list, call a tool per item, accumulate results into a single list output.
Iterate over a list and classify each item with an LLM gate (ai_reasoning).
demonstrates: ai_reasoning activity inside a foreach body
